+# Execution Report
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-233
+AUTHOR: Executor / Claude
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-27_18-19-44+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- crates/physics/src/lib.rs — replaced stale "audit-ledger stub" wording on the `step` surface bullet, rewrote the "Stub status" header as "Local-twin substrate" (drops the false claim that `kernel/{events, audit-ledger}` are still stubs), and clarified the determinism contract item 4 to name `PhysicsInputLedger` as the deterministic source for downstream RNG seeding.
+- crates/physics/src/step.rs — replaced "records inputs to the audit ledger" and the "Audit recording" section header with `PhysicsInputLedger`-anchored wording (now: "records inputs to the physics-domain `PhysicsInputLedger`" + "## Input recording (`PhysicsInputLedger`)"), with an inline cross-ref to the `physics_input_ledger` module docs for the divergence rationale.
+- crates/physics/tests/ledger_boundary.rs — new file; +2 focused integration tests pinning the `PhysicsInputLedger` ↔ generic kernel audit-ledger structural boundary.
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-233_TASK_2026-05-27_18-04-00+0300.md — task packet (created by planner; untouched).
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-233_EXEC_2026-05-27_18-19-44+0300.md — this report.
+STATUS: AWAITING_REVIEW
+
+## Task Packet Reference
+
+`ai_handoffs/ISSUE-233_TASK_2026-05-27_18-04-00+0300.md`
+
+## Pre-Execution Review Consumed
+
+No pre-execution review issued for this dispatch.
+
+## Required Investigation Summary
+
+Per the task packet's "Required Investigation" section:
+
+1. **`crates/physics/src/physics_input_ledger.rs` module docs** read as the source-of-truth for why `PhysicsInputLedger` is not a drop-in for `rge_kernel_audit_ledger::AuditLedger`. The module-level docs already document the divergence in full detail (different domain, different API, no API-compatible swap), justify the rename from the W11-era `AuditLedger` stub, and cross-reference ADR-114's four-substrate-validation amendment table. **No production changes to this file were necessary** — the source-of-truth is already correct. Confirms the task's planner note: "earlier audit history indicates a direct type swap was not API-compatible."
+
+2. **`kernel/audit-ledger/src/{lib,event,ledger}.rs` read for comparison only.** Confirms the structural-difference framing:
+   - `AuditLedger { events: Vec<Event>, cursor: u64 }` — generic event log; each `Event { id: EventId (BLAKE3 over kind_tag+payload), seq, timestamp_ms, kind: EventKind { Action | CadCheckpoint | Custom(String) }, payload: Vec<u8> }`; open `EventKind` (plugin-extensible) + undo/redo cursor.
+   - `PhysicsInputLedger { records: Vec<TickRecord> }`, `TickRecord { tick: u64, inputs: Vec<PhysicsInput> }`, `PhysicsInput = Force | Impulse | JointMotor` — typed per-tick physics-domain only; no opaque payload, no event id, no undo cursor.
+   No edits to `kernel/audit-ledger/**` made or required (also explicitly forbidden by the task's MUST-NOT-edit list and halt conditions).
+
+3. **Stale-wording search across `crates/physics`** for `audit-ledger stub` / `stub` / `kernel/events` / `kernel/audit-ledger` / `AuditLedger`. Hits in MAY-editable files: `lib.rs:19` ("audit-ledger stub"), `lib.rs:27-32` ("Stub status" section names `kernel/audit-ledger` and `kernel/events` as still stubs — both are shipped now), `lib.rs:44` ("from the audit ledger"), `step.rs:5-6 + 16-22` ("audit ledger" / "Audit recording"). Hits in files NOT in the MAY-edit list and intentionally NOT touched: `stubs.rs:5-23` (still calls `kernel/audit-ledger` a stub in the local-twins migration map — out-of-scope), `world.rs:42` and `joint.rs:68` (use "audit ledger" for the stable-id-key narrative, ambiguous but out-of-scope). The latter three are noted as remaining drift that a future tightly-scoped follow-up could close; this dispatch's scope explicitly limits the surface area.
+
+4. **Existing physics-test coverage of plugin resource routing + deterministic replay**:
+   - `crates/physics/src/plugin_adapter.rs::tests` — 8 unit tests including the `physics_plugin_tick_with_world_only_returns_contract_violation_for_input_ledger` (resource_type pinned to `"PhysicsInputLedger"` + World preserved).
+   - `crates/physics/tests/plugin_adapter_smoke.rs` — 6 integration tests including the lifecycle smoke, world-missing and ledger-missing contract violations, multi-tick determinism, and the sibling-panic isolation case.
+   - `crates/physics/tests/deterministic_replay.rs` — 5 tests including `ledger_replay_with_external_force_matches` which exercises per-tick `PhysicsInputLedger` recording + replay end-to-end (the deterministic-replay-over-per-tick-records target the task lists as acceptable).
+   The two new tests in `ledger_boundary.rs` tighten the boundary in directions the existing tests do not cover: foreign-resource non-substitution and typed-record-domain pinning.
+
+## What I Changed
+
+### Source
+
+- `crates/physics/src/lib.rs`: rewrote three doc-comment sites that misrepresented the physics ledger as a "stub" of the kernel audit-ledger. (1) Bullet for `step` module: replaced "audit-ledger stub" with a description that names `PhysicsInputLedger` as the per-tick destination and cross-refs the kernel audit-ledger for contrast. (2) "Stub status" → "Local-twin substrate" section: dropped the false claim that `kernel/audit-ledger` is still a stub (shipped Phase 2.3 / 26 tests / 2026-05-05) and added an explicit paragraph stating that physics deliberately keeps `PhysicsInputLedger` instead of migrating onto the kernel substrate. Also dropped the `kernel/events` stub claim (also shipped). (3) Determinism contract item 4: clarified that downstream RNG must be seeded from `PhysicsInputLedger` or another deterministic upstream source (not "the audit ledger").
+- `crates/physics/src/step.rs`: rewrote the module-level docs to anchor on `PhysicsInputLedger` throughout. Renamed the "## Audit recording" section to "## Input recording (`PhysicsInputLedger`)" and rewrote its body to describe `TickRecord` + typed `PhysicsInput` variants explicitly. Added a leading sentence pointing readers to `physics_input_ledger` module docs for the kernel-substrate divergence rationale.
+
+No public API surface change (no rename, no signature change, no new pub items in `src/`). All edits are doc-comment-only inside `lib.rs` + `step.rs`.
+
+### Tests
+
+- `crates/physics/tests/ledger_boundary.rs::plugin_tick_with_world_and_foreign_resource_still_violates_physics_input_ledger`: stages `World` + a sibling `ForeignGenericPayloadLedger` (a locally-defined test fixture whose surface mirrors a generic event ledger's `Vec<Vec<u8>>` payload shape) into a `PluginContext`. Asserts that `PhysicsPlugin::tick` still surfaces `PluginError::ContractViolation { resource_type: "PhysicsInputLedger" }` — no other typed resource may substitute for the physics-domain ledger — and that the supplied `World` and the foreign sibling are both preserved in the registry (idempotent failure semantics). Pins the "typed-resource routing" boundary that the existing `plugin_adapter_smoke.rs` contract-violation test does not exercise (that test stages only `World`).
+- `crates/physics/tests/ledger_boundary.rs::physics_input_ledger_records_only_typed_physics_domain_inputs`: drives 3 ticks against a small scene (Force on tick 0, Impulse on tick 1, empty tick 2) and asserts `ledger.records` holds exactly 3 `TickRecord`s with monotonically increasing tick indices, that each input is one of the typed `PhysicsInput` variants via an exhaustive match (which makes any future un-typed payload addition a compile error in the test), and that the Force/Impulse counts are exactly 1 each. Pins the "typed-record domain" boundary — `PhysicsInputLedger` is structurally typed, NOT a generic `Vec<u8>`-payload event log.
+
+### Docs
+
+- Out-of-`src/` docs left unchanged. HANDOFF.md persistent-gap line 1575 already correctly states that physics uses `physics_input_ledger::PhysicsInputLedger` as a per-tick domain ledger separate from `kernel/audit-ledger`'s generic event ledger, with a cross-ref to the `physics_input_ledger.rs` module docs. Status.md test counts contain pre-existing drift (the `physics 24` figure already disagreed with the actual count before this dispatch); the planner's "only if ... reconciliation" wording does not call for a reconciliation that wasn't introduced by this dispatch. change.md is append-only chronological and is conventionally updated at queue-commit time, not by the Executor.
+
+## Per-File Summary
+
+`crates/physics/src/lib.rs` (3 doc edits):
+
+1. The `step` surface bullet went from one sentence ending "into the audit-ledger stub" to a five-line description naming `PhysicsInputLedger` as the destination and `rge_kernel_audit_ledger::AuditLedger` as the contrasting generic event ledger, with a cross-ref.
+2. The `## Stub status` section retitled `## Local-twin substrate ([\`stubs\`] module)`. Body rewritten to (a) keep the `components-physics` + `kernel/events` local-twin claim with the same "post-merge swap-over" framing, and (b) add a separate paragraph documenting that `kernel/audit-ledger` has shipped + physics intentionally does not migrate onto it + the type is `PhysicsInputLedger`, not a stub.
+3. Determinism contract item 4 ("RNG ... must be seeded from the audit ledger") expanded to "from the per-tick `PhysicsInputLedger` (or another deterministic upstream source)."
+
+`crates/physics/src/step.rs` (1 doc-block edit covering module preamble lines 1-22):
+
+- Module preamble preface: kept the "Single entry point `physics_step`" sentence but replaced the trailing "records inputs to the audit ledger" with "records inputs to the physics-domain `PhysicsInputLedger`", and added a follow-on paragraph spelling out that the ledger here is `PhysicsInputLedger`, not `rge_kernel_audit_ledger::AuditLedger`, with a cross-ref to the divergence rationale.
+- "## Why fixed-step" section unchanged.
+- "## Audit recording" → "## Input recording (`PhysicsInputLedger`)" with body rewritten to describe `TickRecord` + typed `PhysicsInput` variants (Force / Impulse / JointMotor) explicitly.
+
+`crates/physics/tests/ledger_boundary.rs` (new, ~185L):
+
+- Module-level docs explain the boundary that the file pins and cite `physics_input_ledger.rs` for the divergence rationale.
+- One local fixture type (`ForeignGenericPayloadLedger`, a `Vec<Vec<u8>>` mirror of a generic event-ledger payload shape, declared inside the test file — does NOT add a new physics ledger authority; the type is `#[derive(Debug, Default)]`-only with no impl block beyond the auto-derive).
+- Two tests: typed-resource routing + typed-record domain (see Tests section above).
+
+## Verification Results
+
+- `cargo test -p rge-physics --all-targets --no-fail-fast` → exit 0; **33 passed / 0 failed / 0 ignored** (lib unittests 16 + character_controller 2 + deterministic_replay 5 + falling_cube 2 + ledger_boundary 2 [NEW] + plugin_adapter_smoke 6). The two NEW tests (`plugin_tick_with_world_and_foreign_resource_still_violates_physics_input_ledger` + `physics_input_ledger_records_only_typed_physics_domain_inputs`) both pass cleanly.
+- `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --no-fail-fast` → exit 101; **1 target failed: `-p rge-data --test round_trip`** with 6/9 sub-tests failing on CRLF↔LF line-ending mismatches between the canonical `.ron` serializer (LF) and the on-disk fixtures (CRLF after a Windows git checkout). **Confirmed pre-existing**: stashed all ISSUE-233 changes via `git stash --include-untracked`, re-ran `cargo test -p rge-data --test round_trip` on the clean tree, reproduced the same 6/9 failures with the same `\r\n` vs `\n` diff signature. Restored via `git stash pop`. Failure is environment-side (Windows `core.autocrlf` checkout) and entirely unrelated to ISSUE-233 — physics changes touch no `.ron` fixture, no `rge-data` source, and no serializer.
+- `cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all` → exit 0; 10/10 PASS (`forbidden-dep` / `split-exemption` / `no-utils` / `graph-foundation` / `editor-state-ownership` / `command-bus` / `projection-modules` / `kernel-isolation` / `failure-class` / `snapshot-participate` — including the supplementary `snapshot-participate` check confirming `rge-physics` still impls `SnapshotParticipate`).
+- `.\.ai\dispatch.verify.ps1` → exit 1 (1st run) then exit 101 (2nd run). The 1st run failed at `cargo +nightly fmt --check` for the new `ledger_boundary.rs` file (one multi-line `assert_eq!` formatting nit on the `force_count` assertion). Fixed by reformatting in place. The 2nd run advances past fmt and through every preceding step but fails at `cargo test --workspace --all-targets` with the SAME pre-existing `rge-data --test round_trip` CRLF/LF failure documented above — not introduced by this dispatch.
+
+## Manual Salvage / Human Arbiter Resolution
+
+The original executor correctly halted because the canonical verification gate
+was red on an unrelated Windows line-ending prerequisite. That prerequisite was
+resolved by ISSUE-234 / commit `4bff814`, which pins `.rge-project`,
+`.rge-scene`, and `.rge-prefab` files to LF via `.gitattributes`.
+
+After ISSUE-234 landed on `main`, this branch was rebased onto `origin/main`
+(`d7511b2` on top of `4bff814`). The preserved worktree's checked-out RGE data
+files were refreshed to LF bytes, then the required gates were rerun:
+
+- `cargo test -p rge-data --test round_trip --no-fail-fast` -> exit 0;
+  **9 passed / 0 failed**.
+- `cargo test -p rge-physics --all-targets --no-fail-fast` -> exit 0;
+  **33 passed / 0 failed**.
+- `cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all` -> exit 0;
+  **10/10 lints PASS**.
+- `.\.ai\dispatch.verify.ps1` -> exit 0; full canonical dispatch gate PASS.
+
+The previous `NEEDS_HUMAN` blocker is therefore resolved. The source diff is
+unchanged except for the rebase onto the LF policy commit and this manual
+salvage note.
+
+## Deviations from Task Packet
+
+None — execution stayed strictly within the MAY-edit envelope: only `crates/physics/src/{lib,step}.rs` and the new `crates/physics/tests/ledger_boundary.rs` were edited. MUST-NOT-edit boundaries respected: `kernel/audit-ledger/**`, `kernel/diagnostics/**`, `kernel/plugin-host/**`, `crates/editor-*/`, `crates/cad-*/`, `.github/**`, `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md`, `Cargo.toml`, `Cargo.lock` all untouched. No new public physics ledger type added (the test-local `ForeignGenericPayloadLedger` is a private struct inside the test file with no visibility outside the test target — does not constitute a second physics ledger authority). No dependency changes to `crates/physics/Cargo.toml`. `Cargo.lock` unchanged.
+
+Two stale-wording sites in `crates/physics` were intentionally **NOT** touched because they live outside the MAY-edit list: `crates/physics/src/stubs.rs:5-23` (still calls `kernel/audit-ledger` a stub in the local-twins migration-map header) and `crates/physics/src/world.rs:42` + `crates/physics/src/joint.rs:68` (use ambiguous "audit ledger" phrasing for the stable-id-key narrative). Surfacing these for the Reviewer below.
+
+## Open Questions for Reviewer (Resolved / Deferred)
+
+1. **Pre-existing rge-data CRLF/LF failure blocks `dispatch.verify.ps1` exit 0.** The dispatch produced no `.ron` or serializer change — failure reproduces on a clean tree (verified via stash → re-run → stash-pop). Is this a known "soft FAIL — defer until .gitattributes normalisation" situation, or does it warrant a halt? I left `STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN` so a human can ack the pre-existing breakage before the queue runner publishes. The publish-mode `pr` direction lines up well with that — the PR can be opened for review without merging, and a separate `.gitattributes` (or `* text=auto eol=lf` for `*.ron` under `crates/rge-data/tests/fixtures/`) fix can land alongside.
+2. **Out-of-scope stale wording left behind on purpose.** `stubs.rs:5-23` is the most visibly stale: it claims `kernel/audit-ledger` "is still a stub," which is false. Editing it would have closed the gap more completely, but `stubs.rs` is not in the MAY-edit list. Should a follow-up dispatch widen scope to include `stubs.rs` for the next iteration, or is the planner's intent that `stubs.rs` stay frozen until the broader local-twins removal lands?
+3. **No HANDOFF.md / Status.md / change.md edits.** The planner's wording was conditional ("only if persistent-gap wording or test counts need reconciliation"). I judged HANDOFF.md persistent-gap line 1575 already accurate, Status.md `physics 24` figure to be pre-existing drift not introduced by this dispatch, and change.md a post-commit chronological log. Push back if you wanted a reconciliation entry recorded immediately.
+
+## Worktree State
+
+- Tracked files modified:
+  - `crates/physics/src/lib.rs`
+  - `crates/physics/src/step.rs`
+- Untracked items:
+  - `crates/physics/tests/ledger_boundary.rs` (new test file)
+  - `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-233_TASK_2026-05-27_18-04-00+0300.md` (planner's task packet; created by planner, untouched by Executor)
+  - `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-233_TASK_2026-05-27_18-04-00+0300.meta.json` (planner sidecar; created by planner)
+  - `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-233_EXEC_2026-05-27_18-19-44+0300.md` (this report; will get a sidecar via `new-handoff.ps1 -Finalize`)
+- Branch: `ai-dispatch/ISSUE-233`
+- Last commit before this salvage note: `d7511b2` ai-dispatch ISSUE-233
+  (rebased on `4bff814`, ISSUE-234)
+
+No commit, no push, no merge — per protocol rules.
+

+# Task Packet
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-233
+AUTHOR: Planner / OpenAI Codex
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-27_18-04-00+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- crates/physics/src/lib.rs
+- crates/physics/src/physics_input_ledger.rs
+- crates/physics/src/plugin_adapter.rs
+- crates/physics/src/step.rs
+- crates/physics/tests/*.rs
+- kernel/audit-ledger/src/lib.rs
+- kernel/audit-ledger/src/event.rs
+- kernel/audit-ledger/src/ledger.rs
+- HANDOFF.md
+- Status.md
+- change.md
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-233_*
+- ai_dispatch_logs/log_*ISSUE-233*
+STATUS: OPEN
+
+## Goal
+
+Consolidate the `crates/physics` ledger boundary with the current `kernel/audit-ledger` source truth. This Phase 9 roadmap dispatch should remove stale "kernel audit-ledger stub" wording, verify that `PhysicsInputLedger` remains the intentional per-tick physics-domain input ledger, and add focused tests for the routing/contract behavior that keeps it distinct from the generic kernel event ledger.
+
+## Scope
+
+### MAY edit

+- `crates/physics/src/physics_input_ledger.rs`
